evidence_items.snippet: Immediately following the measurement of post-CFA paw withdrawal latency, TRPV3-YFP transgenic and wild-type mice were injected with ibuprofen (i.p., 40 mg/kg) or vehicle, and 15 min later injected with JNJ-7203212 (40 mg/kg, i.p.) or vehicle.

In contrast, ibuprofen significantly increased the response latency of the TRPV3 overexpressing mice, indicating a greater contribution of prostaglandins to thermal hyperalgesia in the transgenic animals.
The TRPV1 antagonist substantially increased latencies of paw withdrawal from radiant heat in wild-type mice, as well as in TRPV3-YFP transgenic mice.
TRPV3-YFP transgenic mice... exhibited greater thermal nociception than wild-type mice upon pharmacological inhibition of TRPV1.
Coapplication of the nonselective COX inhibitor ibuprofen (100 μm) blocked the stimulated response.
A 5 % w/w ibuprofen gel formulation was used as the model topical formulation.
